raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, I purchased a XXXX  XXXX receiver for {$480.00}. The unit failed XXXX  days after delivery and was sent for warranty repair on XX/XX/XXXX ( XXXX XXXX : XXXX ). On XX/XX/XXXX, the merchant 's agent provided a written XXXX resolution commitment. That deadline expired XX/XX/XXXX with no resolution. The unrepaired unit was returned on XX/XX/XXXX after the merchant 's own stated deadline. 
I opened a billing dispute with Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX ( Case ID : XXXX ). On XX/XX/XXXX, I formally rejected the unit as non-conforming goods under Florida Statute 672.602 and notified the merchant in writing. The unit remains sealed and unopened. 
Citibank denied my dispute on XX/XX/XXXX stating only that " the merchant provided evidence that merchandise was delivered. '' Delivery was never disputed. My dispute reason was " Damaged items '' a defective goods claim under the Fair Credit Billing Act, 15 U.S.C. 1666. Citibank applied the wrong legal standard. 
Additionally, I submitted a full rebuttal with XXXX exhibits to Citibank on XX/XX/XXXX. Citibank 's own portal confirmed receipt. The denial was issued XXXX days later with no reference to my documentation. 
The merchant 's chargeback response claimed I " refused to open the box. '' This mischaracterizes my legal conduct once I formally rejected the goods under FL 672.602, opening the box would have waived my statutory right of rejection. 
Active government complaints : Florida FDACS XXXX XXXX and Florida AG referral . Remedy requested : full refund of {$480.00}.
